description: 10-turn conversation establishing stable identity facts (timezone, feedback style, comms preference). Tests recall of identity-category memories after other turns intervene.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I'm based in Berlin, so I'm on Central European Time.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it — you're in the Europe/Berlin timezone (CET/CEST).

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: When you give me feedback, be direct and blunt. I don't want it softened.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Understood — I'll give you direct, blunt feedback without softening.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Can you check if my 3pm meeting conflicts with anything?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Your 3pm is clear — no conflicts on your calendar.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Also, I strongly prefer async communication. Don't schedule calls unless it's unavoidable.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Noted — I'll default to async (messages, docs) and only propose a call when there's no async path.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: What's my timezone and how do I like feedback?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: You're on Central European Time (Berlin), and you want feedback direct and blunt, not softened.
